old_claim.note: Lithium-Ion Batteries (Rechargeable Power) provides a capability that enables this technology without being the only possible path.

new_claim_absent: No direct dependency remains because grid-scale battery storage covers multiple battery chemistries and is not inherently lithium-ion.

source_shape.source_locator: Abstract of a review of lead-acid battery energy-storage systems for utility applications, including historical utility-scale BESSs.
source_shape.source_claim_summary: The review describes utility battery storage as a lead-acid and broader BESS lineage rather than an exclusively lithium-ion lineage.
source_shape.source_support_rationale: Because grid BESS deployments can be lead-acid, sodium-sulfur, flow, or lithium-ion, a lithium-ion hard prerequisite would incorrectly exclude established non-lithium systems.

ontology_before: Grid-scale battery storage depended on one later dominant chemistry, which overconstrained the field and caused temporal inconsistency after lithium-ion was dated to 1991.
ontology_after: Grid-scale battery storage now uses the graph’s broader electrochemical battery lineage and keeps lithium-ion as an implementation family rather than a prerequisite.
invariant_preserved_or_changed: Graph invariant 2026-06-07-semiconductor-energy-agriculture-chronology.json keeps this removed edge absent and documents the corrected dependency scope.
would_reject_if: The node were narrowed to mean lithium-ion grid battery systems only.
A separate generic stationary-battery node were added and should replace portable_electrochemical_batteries.
A field review showed non-lithium utility BESSs should be excluded from this node.
short_reason: Grid battery storage is chemistry-diverse and should not require lithium-ion.
